game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Spill It:** Players complete physics-based puzzles by dropping a limited number of balls to knock over all glasses on the screen. To play, players tap anywhere on the screen to drop a single ball from a fixed top-center spawn point, with rapid taps allowing for quick successive drops at a rate of approximately 0.2 seconds per ball. Each level features a unique arrangement of 3-10 glasses on various platforms, requiring strategic timing and placement of the 5-10 available balls to achieve a chain reaction or direct impact. A level is won when all glasses are toppled, and lost if the player expends all balls without achieving this objective. Successfully completing levels unlocks the next sequential challenge and awards coins, which can be spent in a shop to unlock cosmetic ball skins and visual themes.
game_info.description: Drop balls from the top of the screen to knock over all the glasses on various platforms. Use physics and chain reactions to your advantage. Complete each level with limited balls to progress through increasingly challenging puzzles. Topple all glasses to win!
game_info.controls:
SPACE: Drop a ball from the top center of the screen
ENTER: Start the game from the start screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause the game
R: Restart and return to the start screen from game over

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic ball dropping mechanics and physics interaction with glasses. Verifies that balls spawn correctly, fall with gravity, and can knock over glasses when they collide with sufficient force.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Drop 3 balls in quick succession from the spawn point at the start of a level. The balls should fall and interact with the glasses and platforms below, demonstrating basic physics behavior.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Balls should spawn at the top center, fall naturally with gravity, bounce off platforms and glasses, and potentially knock over some glasses. The ball count should decrease with each drop. Test is successful if physics interactions work smoothly without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by successfully toppling all glasses in a level. Verifies that the game correctly detects when all glasses are knocked over and transitions to the win state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Automatically drop balls at strategic intervals aimed to knock over all glasses in the level. Continue dropping until either all glasses are toppled or balls run out.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: When all glasses are knocked over (tipped beyond threshold angle), the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state, display victory message, show earned coins, and allow restart. Test is successful when win condition triggers correctly.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition when player runs out of balls before toppling all glasses. Verifies game correctly handles ball depletion and transitions to lose state.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Drop all available balls without successfully knocking over all glasses. Wait for balls to settle and verify the game detects the loss condition.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: After the last ball is dropped and settles (and not all glasses are toppled), the game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state and display appropriate message with restart option. Test is successful when lose condition triggers correctly.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing level progression system. Verifies that completing a level unlocks the next level and that level data persists correctly.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Win the current level by toppling all glasses, then restart and verify the next level is available. Check that level difficulty increases with more glasses or complex platform arrangements.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: After winning a level, the next level should become accessible. Upon restart and starting again, the game should load the next level with different glass and platform configurations. Test is successful if levels progress sequentially.
TEST_4.description: Testing level progression system. Verifies that completing a level unlocks the next level and that level data persists correctly.

TEST_4.expected: After winning a level, the next level should become accessible. Upon restart and starting again, the game should load the next level with different glass and platform configurations. Test is successful if levels progress sequentially.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing pause functionality during active gameplay. Verifies that ESC key correctly pauses and unpauses the game, freezing all physics simulation.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Drop a ball during gameplay, then press ESC to pause while the ball is mid-flight. Verify physics stops. Press ESC again to unpause and verify physics resumes.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: When paused, all Matter.js physics should freeze, balls should stop moving, and a pause overlay should appear. When unpaused, physics should resume from the exact state. Test is successful if pause/unpause works without glitches.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing ball drop cooldown timer. Verifies that balls cannot be dropped faster than the 0.2 second cooldown period to prevent spam.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Attempt to drop multiple balls as rapidly as possible by holding or repeatedly pressing the SPACE key. Monitor the time between actual ball spawns.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Balls should only spawn at maximum rate of one every 0.2 seconds (5 per second), regardless of how rapidly the SPACE key is pressed. Test is successful if cooldown prevents rapid-fire ball spawning.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing collision detection and glass toppling physics. Verifies that glasses require sufficient impact force to tip over and that angle-based toppling detection works correctly.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Drop balls to create various impact scenarios: direct hits, glancing blows, and chain reactions where one glass knocks into another. Observe which impacts successfully topple glasses.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Glasses should only topple when hit with sufficient force or tipped beyond a threshold angle (e.g., 30-45 degrees). Light taps should not topple them. Test is successful if toppling feels realistic and requires meaningful impact.

TEST_7.expected: Glasses should only topple when hit with sufficient force or tipped beyond a threshold angle (e.g., 30-45 degrees). Light taps should not topple them. Test is successful if toppling feels realistic and requires meaningful impact.
